---
title: "Projects"
---

::: {.page-header}
<div class="kicker">Projects</div>
# Research projects and funded work
Applied and methodological projects in health economics, public health policy, preference research and decision support.
:::

::: {.content}
::: {.grid-2}
::: {.card}
### MandEval
Evaluation of vaccine mandates, mandate removals, policy acceptability and future mandate design. The programme combines causal policy evaluation, stated preference methods and decision-support tools.
:::
::: {.card}
### Eye-tracking discrete choice experiments
Experimental work examining how attention to attributes, order effects and information processing shape stated preferences and cost sensitivity.
:::
::: {.card}
### Health preference methods
Methodological studies on feedback, dominance-structured tasks, design robustness, attribute processing and preference heterogeneity.
:::
::: {.card}
### Telehealth preference research
DCE work on access, continuity, modality, waiting time, travel, privacy, data security and out-of-pocket cost.
:::
::: {.card}
### Rare disease care organisation
Patient preference work on care coordination, centres of expertise, innovation access, continuity and service configuration.
:::
::: {.card}
### Benefit-cost analysis tools
Applied decision tools for translating trial, programme and farm-level data into economic metrics including NPV, BCR, ROI and sensitivity results.
:::
:::
:::
